summary: WIN-038.c7 closed via single find_replace patch on data/wins.json:WIN-038.detail_evidence inside the (iv) cylindrical-waveguide-Bessel-modes sub-bullet. The patch removes the curmudgeon-flagged unrigorous Bessel-zero-monotonicity-plus-domain-containment claim (monotonicity in that direction holds for Dirichlet BC, not for the PEC mixed BC of a closed cavity) and substitutes a stronger BC-independent Bessel-zero-gap argument: a flat-cylinder TM mode at 7.83 Hz requires the radial eigenvalue j_{n,m} = 7.83×2π×20,015/299,792 ≈ 3.285, which is not a zero of any J_n; the nearest Bessel zeros j_{0,1}=2.4048 (TM01, 5.73 Hz) and j_{1,1}=3.8317 (TM11, 9.13 Hz) bracket 7.83 Hz with no intermediate Bessel zero, so the flat-cylinder TM spectrum has a 5.73–9.13 Hz gap straddling 7.83 Hz. The replacement additionally satisfies the curmudgeon's recommended remedy (a) by stating the PEC mixed boundary conditions explicitly (Dirichlet on the radial wall, Neumann on top/bottom for E_z) and disclaiming the containment-monotonicity argument as non-trivial under mixed BC; both of the curmudgeon's suggested remedy options are addressed. Kernel-of-truth (Dirichlet domain monotonicity IS a real theorem and the original intuition is correct in spirit) acknowledged explicitly. Anchor uniqueness verified count=1 (length 577 chars). Numerical values verified in EXP authoring session (3.2846 → 3.285; 2.4048 → 5.733 Hz; 3.8317 → 9.134 Hz). Closing sentence ('The dome has not computed or published any coupled-eigenvalue result that lands at 7.83 Hz.') preserved unchanged. After patch applied: close ISS-1374 as fixed-pending-verification. Update tracker EXP-377-pickup-ISS-1374 status → integrated.
